tokenGroup.name: color-palette
tokenGroup.description: The full range of color options in the system. Colors are organized into named hue families, each with a grade scale from 0 (lightest) to 900 (darkest). The 450 grades are reserved for brand usage.

children.name: color-red-pushpin
children.description: The red hue family. Pushpin 450 is the system's primary hero color, used for brand moments and primary product actions.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-0
children.description: Lightest tint of the Pushpin red family. Suitable for large background surfaces and subtle error-state backgrounds.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-50
children.description: Very light tint of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-100
children.description: Light tint of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-200
children.description: Light-medium tint of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-300
children.description: Medium tint of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-400
children.description: Medium shade of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-450
children.description: The system's primary hero color. Reserved for brand usage and primary product actions. Among the least accessible colors in the palette — do not use for functional color pairings without explicit contrast verification.

items.guidance: Reserve this color for brand moments and the primary product action. Do not use for general UI elements.
items.rationale: Pushpin 450 is the system's hero color. Overusing it dilutes its impact and creates accessibility challenges, as the 450 grades have limited contrast pairing options.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-500
children.description: Core red used for error states and critical status indicators.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-600
children.description: Dark shade of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-700
children.description: Deep shade of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-800
children.description: Very dark shade of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-red-pushpin-900
children.description: Darkest shade of the Pushpin red family.

children.name: color-blue-skycicle
children.description: The blue hue family. Used for interactive elements, education surfaces, informational indicators, and shopping experiences.

children.name: color-blue-skycicle-0
children.description: Lightest tint of the Skycicle blue family.

children.name: color-blue-skycicle-100
children.description: Light tint of the Skycicle blue family.

children.name: color-blue-skycicle-300
children.description: Medium tint of the Skycicle blue family. Used as the interactive element color in dark mode.

children.name: color-blue-skycicle-500
children.description: Core blue used for interactive elements, education surfaces, and shopping experiences in light mode.

children.name: color-blue-skycicle-700
children.description: Deep shade of the Skycicle blue family. Used for link text in light mode.

children.name: color-blue-skycicle-900
children.description: Darkest shade of the Skycicle blue family.

children.name: color-gray-roboflow
children.description: The neutral gray family. The foundation of the system's layering model and the most commonly used color family in both light and dark modes.

children.name: color-gray-roboflow-50
children.description: Near-white neutral. Used for subtle background differentiation.

children.name: color-gray-roboflow-200
children.description: Light gray. Used for secondary backgrounds and borders.

children.name: color-gray-roboflow-500
children.description: Mid-gray. Used for subtle text, default borders, and disabled states in light mode. The minimum gray that meets WCAG AA contrast on white for large text.

children.name: color-gray-roboflow-700
children.description: Dark gray.

children.name: color-white-mochimalist
children.description: The white value. Used as the default background surface in light mode and as inverse text in dark mode.

children.name: color-white-mochimalist-0
children.description: Pure white.

children.name: color-black-cosmicore
children.description: The near-black value. Used as the default text color and background surface in dark mode.

children.name: color-black-cosmicore-900
children.description: Near-black. The darkest color in the system — used for default text in light mode and the default background in dark mode.

items.guidance: The 450 colors are reserved for brand usage. Do not use 450 grades for functional UI elements without explicit accessibility verification.
items.rationale: The 450 grades are among the least accessible colors in the palette. They work best within larger brand moments and are not suitable for functional color pairings.

items.guidance: Use the darkGray text color on any background color at grade 400 or below. Use white text on grade 500 and above.
items.rationale: Ensures WCAG 2.1 AA contrast compliance for text placed on palette colors.

items.guidance: Do not use base palette tokens directly in product code. Use semantic tokens that reference these base values instead.
items.rationale: Semantic tokens adapt automatically across color modes. Direct use of base palette tokens bypasses the theming system and will produce incorrect results in dark mode.
